definition: VP's CMP_MAP operand-distance gradient drives a numeric or character-class value past a magnitude boundary or into/out of a range (inequality gate, threshold, or character-class test); the gradient preserves inputs that are numerically or bitwise closer to the boundary, ratcheting toward a threshold crossing that naive/cmplog edge feedback cannot sense.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP intercepts the integer comparison at line 827 and writes an operand-distance bucket; inputs whose TagCount is numerically closer to crossing MAX_TABLE_TAG are retained, providing a gradient that drives TagCount above the threshold while naive's edge-only feedback cannot.
signature.one_line: VP CMP_MAP numeric gradient drives TagCount above MAX_TABLE_TAG threshold

analysis.summary_one_line: The 4-byte big-endian TagCount field read at offset just after the header must exceed MAX_TABLE_TAG.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The TagCount value parsed by _cmsReadUInt32Number at line 826 (a 4-byte big-endian integer in the tag-directory region of the ICC profile) must be numerically larger than MAX_TABLE_TAG so the comparison at line 827 takes its TRUE side and reaches the 'Too many tags' error path.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile resolves the branch in all 10 trials (W shows T=9 F=0 at line 827) and reaches the W-only error block at lines 829-831; the CMP at line 827 gives value_profile an operand-distance gradient that drives TagCount upward until it crosses MAX_TABLE_TAG.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks in all 10 trials (L shows T=0 F=10 at line 827) and instead falls into the tag-reading loop (L-only lines 835-885 with L=T=181 iterations at line 836), meaning its mutated TagCount values stayed at or below MAX_TABLE_TAG; pure havoc never landed a large-enough value.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile intercepts the integer comparison at line 827 and writes an operand-distance bucket into CMP_MAP; an input whose TagCount is numerically closer to crossing MAX_TABLE_TAG adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is retained, providing a gradient that naive's edge-only feedback lacks, so value_profile climbs to TagCount > MAX_TABLE_TAG while naive cannot.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is the TagCount > MAX_TABLE_TAG comparison whose TRUE side is globally blocked.
TagCount is read from the input as a 4-byte unsigned integer immediately before the blocker.
value_profile is the winner and naive is the loser of the single decisive pair.
value_profile resolves the branch in all 10 trials while naive blocks in all 10.
At line 827 the winner takes the TRUE side and the loser takes the FALSE side.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 827  T=9 F=0  T=0 F=10  if (TagCount > MAX_TABLE_TAG) {

evidence_trail.claim: The winner reaches the 'Too many tags' error block while the loser enters the tag-reading loop.
value_profile feeds an operand-distance bucket from intercepted CMPs into CMP_MAP, retaining inputs that get closer to matching.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has only per-edge hit-count feedback with no CMP introspection.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
**Feedback**: per-edge hit-count bucket; a new bucket triggers a
corpus-add (LibAFL `MaxMapFeedback` over the edge map).

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating TagCount > MAX_TABLE_TAG on a value read from input where value_profile fails to resolve the TRUE side significantly more often than naive across trials would refute the CMP_MAP-gradient attribution.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: MAX_TABLE_TAG's concrete numeric value is not shown in the prompt, so the exact distance value_profile's gradient must climb is inferred from the comparison being an inequality rather than measured.

signature.mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance buckets reward inputs whose multi-byte greenx fixed-point operand creeps progressively toward and past the 100000 magnitude threshold, building a gradient that cmplog's literal substitution cannot follow.
signature.one_line: VP gradient walks multi-byte fixed-point operand past magnitude threshold

analysis.summary_one_line: A parsed cHRM green-x chromaticity value driven out of [0, PNG_FP_1=100000] so the greenx>PNG_FP_1 guard takes its true (reject) branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Input must reach png_XYZ_from_xy with a green chromaticity field whose fixed-point value satisfies greenx<0 OR greenx>PNG_FP_1 (100000 = 0x000186A0), i.e. the multi-byte greenx operand must be pushed above the 100000 magnitude threshold so the line-1288 guard takes its true side and returns 1.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The value_profile_cmplog winner reaches line 1288 and takes the true side (W shows T=1 F=0 at L1288 while the loser shows T=0 F=40), resolves the branch in 9/10 trials, and accumulates 56 hits versus the loser's 3 — its chromaticity bytes land greenx outside the [0,PNG_FP_1] window.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The cmplog loser only ever takes the false side at L1288 (T=0 F=40), blocks in 8/10 trials, and never drives greenx above the 100000 threshold; it stalls 21.10h on the branch with only 3 hits, so its green chromaticity stays inside the in-range window.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Both fuzzers carry the same input-to-state substitution mutator (cmplog and vpc are identical except for value_profile), so the comparison-solving mutator is held constant; the winning technique is value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient. The comparison greenx>PNG_FP_1 is a magnitude inequality against the multi-byte constant 100000 (0x000186A0) that literal-substitution mutation cannot directly solve, but the CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance buckets reward inputs whose greenx operand creeps progressively closer to and past 100000, giving the gradient that lets vpc overshoot the threshold where plain cmplog cannot.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the inequality guard greenx>PNG_FP_1 at png.c:1288 whose true side is globally blocked.
The single decisive pair is value_profile_cmplog beating cmplog with the technique delta being value_profile.
The winner resolves 9/10 and the loser blocks 8/10 with a large hitcount gap.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=56  loser=3

evidence_trail.claim: The winner takes the true side of the line-1288 guard while the loser never does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: T=1 F=0  T=0 F=40  if (xy->greenx < 0 || xy->greenx > PNG_FP_1) return 1;  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The loser stalls far longer on the branch than the winner.
value_profile_cmplog's feedback adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets on top of the edge and CMP-buffer signals.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
edge counters,
per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets.

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog and value_profile_cmplog share the same I2SRandReplace mutator stack, so I2S is not the differentiator.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on an out-of-range multi-byte greenx>100000 magnitude inequality where value_profile_cmplog does NOT outperform cmplog (no dose-response separation), which would show the CMP_MAP gradient is not what drives the divergence.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The exact greenx byte offsets that the winner pushes out of range are not isolated in the BYTE DIFF (the cHRM chromaticity field is buried in a later chunk), so the attribution rests on the value_profile delta plus the inequality-against-100000 source shape rather than a pinpointed seed-byte gradient.

signature.mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance buckets reward inputs whose redy integer value approaches the PNG_FIXED_ERROR sentinel bitwise, accumulating near-misses and walking arithmetic mutation to the exact 0x7fffffff value that cmplog's edge-only feedback treats as undifferentiated.
signature.one_line: VP CMP_MAP gradient walks redy integer toward PNG_FIXED_ERROR sentinel

analysis.summary_one_line: A short cHRM-bearing PNG reaching png_handle_cHRM whose redy fixed-point at buf+12 parses to the PNG_FIXED_ERROR sentinel (0x7fffffff).

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same structural requirement: a cHRM-bearing PNG whose redy fixed-point at buf+12 evaluates to PNG_FIXED_ERROR, reaching the L1280 equality comparison so it can resolve true.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 8/10 and takes the L1280 true side (hitcount 4 on the branch), aided by the CMP_MAP gradient that gives partial-progress credit on the integer comparison xy.redy == PNG_FIXED_ERROR, letting Hamming-distance-closer inputs survive in the corpus and be refined toward the exact 0x7fffffff value.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (lacking the value_profile CMP_MAP feedback) gets no partial-progress signal on the redy comparison, resolves only 1/10 with hitcount 0 on the branch, and stays blocked an average 22.30h.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: the one-technique delta vs cmplog is value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient: per-CMP Hamming/prefix-distance buckets reward inputs whose redy value is bitwise closer to PNG_FIXED_ERROR, so the corpus accumulates near-misses and arithmetic mutation walks them to the exact sentinel — cmplog's edge-only feedback treats every not-yet-equal redy as identical.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is the redy==PNG_FIXED_ERROR equality test, true side globally blocked.
redy is parsed from the cHRM 32-byte buffer at offset 12.
Reaching the comparison requires a 32-byte cHRM chunk arriving before IDAT/PLTE.
naive wins the I2S-delta pair over cmplog with strong probability divergence.
naive resolved 9/10, cmplog blocked 9/10 on this branch.
Winner invokes png_handle_cHRM far more often than the loser (64 vs 20).
The winner takes the L1280 true side 12 times while the loser takes it zero times.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1280  T=12 F=45  T=0 F=19  xy.redy   == PNG_FIXED_ERROR ||  <-- BLOCKER

naive winning seeds are short (656-1314 bytes).
cmplog blocking seeds are large (up to 8759 bytes).
naive has no I2S and relies on havoc + token mutation throughput.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile_cmplog wins the value_profile-delta pair over cmplog.
value_profile_cmplog resolved 8/10 with hitcount 4 on the branch.
vpc adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets over cmplog's edge feedback.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
edge counters,
per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets.

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog feedback is the same edge-bucket signal as naive, with no value-profile gradient.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on redy==PNG_FIXED_ERROR where cmplog resolves as fast as or faster than naive and value_profile_cmplog, i.e. adding CMP interception does NOT slow reaching the sentinel — would refute both the throughput-tax (pair 1) and the CMP_MAP-gradient-helps (pair 2) attributions.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: I cannot directly see the 4 bytes at cHRM-data offset 12 in the seed dumps (the cHRM chunk lies beyond the 64-byte window), so the claim that the redy field decodes to exactly 0x7fffffff is inferred from the L1280 true-side hitcounts rather than observed bytes.

signature.mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP intercepts the masked integer comparison (c & 0xFC00) == 0xD800; inputs whose masked operand is Hamming-closer to 0xD800 add a new distance bucket and are kept, gradient-climbing to the surrogate equality that naive's edge feedback cannot sense.
signature.one_line: VP CMP_MAP gradient climbs masked 16-bit operand toward 0xD800 surrogate constant

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must be routed to the UTF16LE decoder and contain a 16-bit code unit whose top six bits equal the 0xD800 high-surrogate pattern.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A 16-bit code unit c reaching the comparison (c & 0xFC00) == 0xD800, i.e. a value whose top six bits move toward the 0xD800 constant; satisfying it requires the masked operand to equal 0xD800 exactly.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile resolved 9/10 and drove the branch true with the highest hitcount (96 vs loser 4); the integer-CMP at line 533 is intercepted into the CMP_MAP, so inputs whose (c & 0xFC00) value is closer to 0xD800 add a new distance bucket and are kept, gradient-climbing to the exact match.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Plain naive has no CMP_MAP, so an input that gets c & 0xFC00 near but not equal to 0xD800 yields no new edge and is discarded; naive stayed blocked 8/10 with branch true count 0 and average hitcount 4.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile adds an integer-CMP interception writing a (PC, operand-distance bucket) entry into the CMP_MAP; a Hamming-distance-closer (c & 0xFC00) vs 0xD800 pair is preserved as corpus, giving a gradient toward the equality that naive's plain edge feedback cannot sense.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the UTF-16LE high-surrogate test in UTF16LEToUTF8.
naive_ngram4 resolved every trial while naive was mostly blocked.
The loser naive resolved only one trial and was blocked in eight.

evidence_trail.claim: Winning seeds carry a UTF-16 byte-order-mark-like prefix and 0x00-interleaved code units.
The loser-blocking seed is dominated by ASCII space runs that cannot form a 0xD8xx code unit.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: Attributing the naive_ngram4 win specifically to 4-edge path coverage (rather than to incidental extra corpus diversity) is the least directly evidenced claim, since no mutation_op lineage was inspected for the n-gram seeds.

signature.mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback scores chained hex-digit range comparisons, preserving inputs whose bytes approach then cross the hex-digit boundary, enabling incremental construction of a &#x reference with a non-hex terminator.
signature.one_line: Gradient walks bytes through hex-digit range to produce invalid hex-charref

analysis.summary_one_line: A hexadecimal character reference (&#x...) whose digit run is terminated by a non-hex-digit byte, driving xmlParseCharRef into the invalid-hex-charref error arm.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A hex character-reference construct that enters xmlParseCharRef's hex loop (lines 2327-2332) and then presents a byte that fails ALL three hex-digit range tests ([0-9], [a-f], [A-F]), so control reaches the else arm at line 2333 which calls xmlFatalErr(ctxt, XML_ERR_INVALID_HEX_CHARREF, NULL) and lands on case 256.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile winner seeds carry char-ref/markup material (e.g. '(#PCD', '-UCS-4', ';link9href') and the per-role overlay marks the hex-digit range comparisons and the else->xmlFatalErr arm (lines 2327, 2329, 2331, 2333-2337) as [W] winner-only, and the blocker case 256 itself as [W]; the branch true side is taken 12 times for W but 0 times for L.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive loser seeds are well-formed '<?xml version="1.0"?>' DOCTYPE/SYSTEM documents that never assemble a hex char-ref terminated by a non-hex byte; at line 256 the loser takes the false side 147 times and the true side 0 times, so the invalid-hex-charref arm is never reached.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback scores the chained hex-digit range comparisons (RAW vs '0'/'9'/'a'/'f'/'A'/'F') in xmlParseCharRef, preserving inputs that drive operands toward and then just past the hex-digit boundary; this gradient lets value_profile incrementally construct a &#x reference whose terminating byte fails the range tests, while naive's edge-only feedback gives no signal on how close a byte is to a hex digit and cannot stage that construction.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is the XML_ERR_INVALID_HEX_CHARREF switch case at parser.c:256 inside xmlFatalErr, with the true side globally blocked.
value_profile resolves the branch in all 10 trials while naive blocks in 9 of 10, a prob_div of 0.90.
The blocker is reached from xmlParseCharRef's hex loop when a byte fails all three hex-digit range tests, hitting the else arm that calls xmlFatalErr with XML_ERR_INVALID_HEX_CHARREF.
The winner-only overlay marks the hex-digit range comparison that gates entry to the fatal-error arm.
At the blocker line the winner takes the true side 12 times while the loser never takes it (0 true, 147 false).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: T=12 F=20  T=0 F=147  case XML_ERR_INVALID_HEX_CHARREF:

evidence_trail.claim: Winner seeds carry char-ref/markup fragments rather than clean well-formed documents.
Loser seeds are well-formed XML declaration / DOCTYPE documents that do not build an invalid hex char-ref.
value_profile's mechanism is CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback that preserves inputs whose operand pair is closer to matching than any seen before.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile uses no I2S substitution mutator, so the divergence is attributable to the CMP_MAP feedback technique only.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with a single isolated hex char-ref loop where value_profile and naive resolve the invalid-hex-charref arm at indistinguishable rates (no value_profile advantage), which would refute that CMP_MAP gradient feedback on the hex-digit range comparisons is the divergence driver.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The seed bytes show char-ref/markup material but no offset shows a literal '&#x' digit-then-non-digit run, so the exact byte path into xmlParseCharRef's else arm is inferred from the [W] overlay rather than read directly from a winner seed.

signature.mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP distance feedback on digit-range comparisons rewards bytes progressively closer to valid decimal digits, driving longer digit runs that eventually push the loop counter past 20.
signature.one_line: CMP_MAP digit-range gradient lengthens run until count exceeds 20

analysis.summary_one_line: Take true side requires a decimal char-ref (&#) whose digit run drives the count++ loop past 20 iterations.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A decimal character reference (`&#` then a long ASCII-digit run) where each digit satisfies the `(RAW >= '0') && (RAW <= '9')` range comparison, so the loop keeps advancing through enough digits to push count past 20.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The loop body compares each byte against the '0'..'9' range at L2359; value_profile's CMP_MAP records how close each byte is to those bounds, so inputs whose bytes approach valid digits are preserved and extended, lengthening the digit run. The value_profile pair resolves 9/10 with branch hitcount 8 versus the loser's 1.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive's loser seeds reach the loop only 4 times (L2352 T=4) and average hitcount 1 on the branch; without CMP-distance feedback naive gets no gradient toward filling the run with in-range digit bytes, so the loop exits early via the invalid-char break before count exceeds 20.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile adds per-CMP CMP_MAP distance buckets on the digit-range comparisons at L2359, giving a Hamming/prefix gradient that rewards bytes closer to valid decimal digits. This drives progressively longer digit runs that eventually trip the count++>20 reset branch — a CMP_MAP gradient effect.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the count overflow guard inside the decimal char-ref loop, and its true side is globally blocked.

naive_ctx winner drives the loop header True 111 times while the naive loser reaches it only 4 times.
The winner takes the count-overflow true side 9 times while the loser takes it 0 times.

evidence_trail.exact_quote:

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with the same count++>20 decimal-loop where naive resolves the true side as often as naive_ctx and value_profile across trials, showing neither context-sensitive coverage nor CMP_MAP gradient is needed to grow the digit run past 20 iterations.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff seeds are single-provenance (naive_ctx/naive) so the value_profile hypothesis leans on the loop's digit-range comparison plus hitcount divergence rather than on a value_profile-stored seed.

signature.mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback on integer character-class comparisons inside xmlIsDocNameChar rewards inputs whose leading byte edges toward a rejected-class value, letting the fuzzer incrementally synthesize an attribute value with a non-NameChar leading character.
signature.one_line: VP gradient evolves leading byte outside NameChar acceptance set via char-class CMP feedback

analysis.summary_one_line: Reach an ATTLIST NMTOKENS/ENUMERATION attribute whose validated value begins with a non-NameChar, so xmlIsDocNameChar fails and the function returns early.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A DTD declaring an attribute of type NMTOKENS or ENUMERATION (so xmlValidateNmtokensValueInternal is invoked) whose attribute value's first non-blank character is NOT a doc NameChar, driving the !xmlIsDocNameChar test true and the early return(0) at line 3780.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile/value_profile_cmplog winners resolve the branch (10/10 and 8/10) and hit the true side (W: line 3780 [W], L3779 T=29 F=0), i.e. they synthesise an attribute value whose leading char is rejected by xmlIsDocNameChar; the CMP_MAP gradient over the character-class comparisons inside xmlIsDocNameChar lets value_profile incrementally evolve a byte that lands in the rejected class.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive and cmplog losers stay blocked (8/10) and instead take the false side, falling into the xmlIsDocNameChar acceptance loop at line 3782 onward (L marks 3782-3810, L3779 T=0 F=58); their attribute values keep starting with an accepted NameChar so the true branch is never reached.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile (and value_profile_cmplog) add a CMP_MAP keyed by operand-distance buckets; the integer-CMP comparisons that implement xmlIsDocNameChar's character-class test produce new distance buckets as a candidate byte approaches a value outside the accepted NameChar set, preserving intermediate inputs as corpus and letting the fuzzer climb to a rejected leading character. The single-technique delta in both pairs is value_profile (vpc vs cmplog and vp vs naive both add only the CMP_MAP gradient).

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the !xmlIsDocNameChar test in xmlValidateNmtokensValueInternal and the globally blocked side is the true branch.
The function is reached only for NMTOKENS or ENUMERATION attribute types via the caller switch.

evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L3779  T=29 F=0  T=0 F=58  if (!xmlIsDocNameChar(doc, val))  <-- BLOCKER

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose attribute-value first-character rejection is gated by a single equality/character-class CMP that value_profile resolves no faster than naive (no per-execution CMP_MAP distance-bucket progress), so value_profile shows no resolution advantage over naive at this branch.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: Whether the value_profile advantage comes from a true CMP_MAP distance gradient over xmlIsDocNameChar's character-class comparisons rather than from incidental faster corpus growth is inferred from mechanism text, not directly observed at the CMP level.
